ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'white wine' class (FOODON_03301737); no specific Friulano DOC entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LK covers white wine generically; no Friulano DOC-specific code exists in the published FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 53410008 covers wine as a substance; no Friulano-specific concept exists.

serving.description: 1 standard glass (150 ml) or 1 bottle (750 ml)
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Friulano DOC white wine is approximately 150 ml (one glass), equivalent to roughly 150 g; a full bottle contains 750 ml.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Wine, table, white (FDC ID 174837)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for generic white table wine used as the closest approved-source equivalent for Friulano DOC. Friulano DOC is a dry Italian white wine with comparable macronutrient profile to generic white table wine. Alcohol content (~12–13% ABV) is consistent with USDA white wine reference. Values adapted to reflect typical dry white wine composition. Input per_100g values (energy 80 kcal, carbohydrate 3 g, sodium 1 mg) are consistent with USDA white wine data and have been retained with minor corrections to align with USDA SR Legacy reference values.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: Alcoholic beverages including dry white wine have a very low GI because ethanol does not raise blood glucose significantly; residual carbohydrate (2.6 g/100 g) is minimal and fermented sugars are largely consumed. The University of Sydney GI Database lists wine as having a negligible glycaemic response, often approximated as a low single-digit GI value. GI is set to 5 (low) and GL per 150 ml serving is 0.0. This is consistent with peer-reviewed GI literature (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). A GI of 0 is typically reserved for pure fats or proteins with no carbohydrate content.

hypertension.key_factor: Alcohol content (~12–13% ABV) directly raises blood pressure and antagonises antihypertensive medications
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid alcoholic beverages including this wine, as even moderate alcohol consumption acutely elevates systolic and diastolic blood pressure and reduces the efficacy of antihypertensive agents; the low sodium content (5 mg/100 g) does not mitigate the alcohol-mediated pressor effect.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Alcohol impairs hepatic gluconeogenesis and causes unpredictable hypoglycaemia in patients on insulin or sulfonylureas
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this wine because alcohol inhibits hepatic glucose output, increasing hypoglycaemia risk in those on insulin or sulfonylureas; if consumed, it must never be taken on an empty stomach and blood glucose must be monitored closely; residual carbohydrate (2.6 g/100 g) also contributes to glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Alcohol raises triglycerides, contributes to arrhythmia risk (holiday heart syndrome), and interacts with anticoagulant and antiplatelet medications
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with established cardiovascular disease should avoid this wine because regular alcohol consumption raises serum triglycerides, increases atrial fibrillation risk, and potentiates the effect of anticoagulants such as warfarin; the absence of saturated fat and low sodium are not sufficient to offset the cardiovascular risks of alcohol.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Alcohol is pro-inflammatory and contraindicated with methotrexate and most RA biologics due to hepatotoxicity risk
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis must avoid this wine because alcohol amplifies systemic inflammation and is contraindicated with methotrexate (severe hepatotoxicity risk) and most biologic DMARDs; patients should consult their rheumatologist before consuming any alcohol while on RA medications.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Friulano DOC White Wine (San Zuan, Friuli Colli Orientali, Ronco del Gnemiz, 2014) is a premium Italian dry white wine produced in the Friuli Colli Orientali Denominazione di Origine Controllata (DOC) zone in Friuli-Venezia Giulia, northeastern Italy. It is made from the Friulano grape variety (historically known as Tocai Friulano), fermented and aged using traditional winemaking methods. The wine presents an intense straw-yellow colour with golden reflections and offers complex aromas of white flowers, peach, pear, apricot, and melon. On the palate it is full-bodied, round, elegant, soft, and persistent, with a dry finish typical of the Friulano style. At approximately 12–13% ABV, it is an alcoholic beverage. Per 100 ml, it provides approximately 82 kcal, 0.1 g protein, 0 g fat, and 2.6 g carbohydrate (predominantly residual fermented sugars). It contains sulphites as a preservative. Clinically, this wine is contraindicated for patients with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, rheumatoid arthritis, and established cardiovascular disease due to its alcohol content. It is vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free. A standard serving is 150 ml (one glass); a full bottle is 750 ml.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy generic white table wine (FDC ID 174837), which is the closest approved-source equivalent. No approved-source entry exists specifically for Friulano DOC. The macronutrient profile of a dry Italian white wine is well-characterised and consistent with USDA reference data, supporting medium confidence. GI is estimated from peer-reviewed literature on alcoholic beverages.
notes: 1. ZERO-MACRO JUSTIFICATION: fat_g is naturally zero for dry white wine — wine contains no dietary fat by composition, confirmed per USDA FoodData Central white wine reference (FDC ID 174837). dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as wine is a fermented liquid with no plant fibre. protein_g is reported as 0.1 g (trace) per USDA reference, not zero. 2. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA FDC white wine data, not -1) as USDA reports these as 0 for white table wine. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero). 3. SOURCE ADAPTATION: Input record cited USDA FoodData Central generically; this record uses USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 174837 (white table wine) as the specific approved-source equivalent. Input energy value of 80 kcal/100 g has been updated to 82 kcal/100 g per USDA reference; carbohydrate updated from 3.0 g to 2.6 g per USDA; sodium updated from 1 mg to 5 mg per USDA. 4. COUNTRY OF ORIGIN: Input incorrectly listed 'Singapore' as country of origin; corrected to 'Friuli-Venezia Giulia, Italy'. 5. ETHNIC TAGS: Input ethnic_tags included 'Pasta', 'Pizza', 'Chicken Dish' which are irrelevant to wine; these have been removed. 6. ALCOHOL: This is an alcoholic beverage (~12–13% ABV). Alcohol content is not captured in the per_100g nutrient fields as the template does not include an alcohol_g field; energy from alcohol is reflected in the energy_kcal value. 7. GI NOTE: GI is set to 5 (low) for dry white wine as ethanol does not raise blood glucose significantly; this is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature. GL per 150 ml serving = 0.0. 8. ONTOLOGY: FoodOn FOODON_03301737 and FoodEx2 A00LK are generic white wine codes; no Friulano DOC-specific ontology codes exist in published catalogues as of 2024. 9. ALIASES CONSISTENCY: All strings in aliases_by_language (english and romanized arrays) appear verbatim in the flat aliases array. The romanized entry 'Friulano DOC, San Zuan Friuli Colli Orientali, Ronco del Gnemiz' has been added to the flat aliases array to ensure full consistency.
